subtaskTitle: Establish a personal rule: never share anything you haven't verified

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "It is enough of a lie for a person to narrate everything he hears" (Muslim).

resolved.note: While the Sahih Muslim Table of Contents lists a chapter on stating hadith after verification (Chapter 17), the corresponding text and hadith number are not contained in the provided excerpts.

subtaskTitle: Pray istikharah with sincerity and an open heart

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) taught his companions istikharah for all their affairs (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari (Volume 2, Book 21, Number 263): 'The Prophet (p.b.u.h) used to teach us the way of doing Istikhara... in all matters as he taught us the Suras of the Quran.'

subtaskTitle: Read "Thinking, Fast and Slow" by Daniel Kahneman or a comparable text

payload.sentence: Understanding these systems is a modern form of self-knowledge — and the Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "The wise person is one who accounts for himself" (Tirmidhi).

resolved.note: The specific quote regarding a wise person accounting for himself (attributed to Tirmidhi) is not found in the provided sources.

subtaskTitle: Share your findings with a friend or study partner for mutual accountability

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "The believer is a mirror to his brother" (Abu Dawud).

resolved.note: The specific quote regarding a believer being a mirror to his brother (attributed to Abu Dawud) is not found in the provided sources.

payload.sentence: The Prophet (peace be upon him) said: "Convey from me, even if it is one ayah" (Bukhari).

resolved.note: Recorded in Sahih al-Bukhari (Volume 4, Book 56, Number 667): 'Convey (my teachings) to the people even if it were a single sentence [ayah]...'
